_meta.note: First real regression pins for the data-driven large-format newspaper layout engine. Unlike a scientific-results manuscript this exemplar reports deterministic LAYOUT / RENDER statistics: manuscript/04_reproducibility.md explicitly names 'the page count, the trim dimensions, the figure count' as its quantitative claims and points at data/claim_ledger.yaml as their register. Each pin re-derives its value from src (load_edition, load_newspaper_config(...).geometry(), figures.generate_all) exactly as the real render path (engine.build_and_render) does -- never by copying a number from the manuscript prose. Trim is bound through the config loader (edition.yaml declares page: tabloid) rather than the bare PageGeometry default so it exercises the same path the renderer uses. No mocks: real YAML content dir + real Pillow/Matplotlib figure generation.

layout_edition_page_count.reason: Number of printed pages in the deterministic edition, re-derived from the content data model: content/edition.yaml lists exactly 12 page files and Edition.page_count == len(pages). This is the manuscript's headline 'twelve-page' claim and the claim_ledger 'page-count' entry. Not copied from prose.

layout_trim_width_pt.reason: Page trim WIDTH in points via the real render path: load_newspaper_config(content).geometry().width. edition.yaml declares page: tabloid, so config maps it to (11in, 17in) and PageGeometry stores 11*72 = 792.0pt. This is the same geometry the Canvas is opened with in engine.render_edition, and the claim_ledger 'page-trim-width-pt' entry.

layout_trim_height_pt.reason: Page trim HEIGHT in points via the real render path: load_newspaper_config(content).geometry().height. Tabloid maps to 17in, so PageGeometry stores 17*72 = 1224.0pt. Matches the claim_ledger 'page-trim-height-pt' entry and the Canvas pagesize used by engine.render_edition.

layout_generated_figure_count.reason: Total figures the standard edition generator emits: figures.generate_all writes 6 halftone scenes + 4 color display-ad graphics + 3 charts = 13 PNGs and returns their paths. This is the manuscript's 'figure count' claim and the claim_ledger 'figure-count' entry; re-derived by actually running the real Pillow/Matplotlib generators into a temp dir (no mocks).
layout_generated_figure_count.script: PYTHONPATH=<newspaper>/src .venv/bin/python -c 'import tempfile; from newspaper import figures; d=tempfile.mkdtemp(); print(len(figures.generate_all(d)))'

layout_edition_page_count.manuscript_section: manuscript/04_reproducibility.md / Reproducibility (line 17: "exactly twelve pages"); manuscript/00_abstract.md ("twelve-page, large-format newspaper")
layout_edition_page_count.claim_text: renders ... a valid PDF of exactly twelve pages at the correct trim with no over-set page

layout_trim_width_pt.manuscript_section: manuscript/04_reproducibility.md / Reproducibility ("the trim dimensions"); data/claim_ledger.yaml / page-trim-width-pt
layout_trim_width_pt.claim_text: the page trim ... US Tabloid / Ledger, 11in x 17in -> 792pt wide (page-trim-width-pt: 792)

layout_trim_height_pt.manuscript_section: manuscript/04_reproducibility.md / Reproducibility ("the trim dimensions"); data/claim_ledger.yaml / page-trim-height-pt

layout_generated_figure_count.manuscript_section: manuscript/04_reproducibility.md / Reproducibility ("the figure count"); data/claim_ledger.yaml / figure-count
layout_generated_figure_count.claim_text: halftone "engraving" figures and ... charts ... 6 halftone scenes + 4 color ad graphics + 3 charts = 13 (figure-count: 13)
